event_name: Shepherd Creative Arts Presents: Honk! JR
venue: Sewell Mill Library and Cultural Center, Marietta
blurb: Shepherd Creative Arts is bringing Honk! JR to the Sewell Mill Library and Cultural Center this Friday — the musical retelling of the Ugly Duckling, performed by young performers for a family crowd. It runs about 90 minutes and it's right here in East Cobb. If you have younger kids at home or grandkids visiting over the holiday weekend, this is an easy, local, low-pressure night out. Check Eventbrite for tickets before the weekend fills up.
scoring_notes: Legitimate local youth theater production at a known East Cobb venue. Lower scores because the audience skews toward families with young children, and East Cobb's demographic mix trends toward teens, college-age kids, and empty nesters. Still a real event worth including for the right segment of the readership.

event_name: Norman Connors and The Starship Band Memorial Day Event
blurb: Norman Connors has been a fixture in jazz and R&B since the 1970s, and he's bringing The Starship Band to Perfect Note Atlanta on Memorial Day. Smooth jazz, soul, and what the event itself calls "grown-folk vibes" — which is an accurate description and a selling point. Perfect Note is a small, intimate room, which means this will feel like a real show, not a concert hall. If you're looking for something to do on the Monday holiday that isn't a cookout, this fits. Doors at 8pm.
scoring_notes: Norman Connors is a legitimate jazz and R&B name with real history, and the Memorial Day timing is a natural hook. The smooth jazz and soul genre skews slightly older and toward a specific audience subset rather than the broad East Cobb demographic. Good for the right reader, lower universal audience match.

event_name: Global Running Day with Big Peach East Cobb and ASICS
venue: Big Peach Running Co., East Cobb/Marietta
blurb: Big Peach Running Co. on Johnson Ferry is celebrating Global Running Day with a free event alongside ASICS this Wednesday evening. East Cobb has a serious running community, and this is the kind of neighborhood-level thing that's easy to walk into — no race bib, no registration fee, just people who like to move. Details on what's happening inside the event are still coming together, but it's free, it's local, and it's a good excuse to get out of the house mid-week. Check their Eventbrite page for updates.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews active and health-conscious, and Big Peach East Cobb is a known local anchor. Global Running Day is a real annual hook. Moderate uniqueness since it's a brand-sponsored community event rather than a one-of-a-kind experience, but the hyper-local venue and free admission make it a solid fit.

blurb: Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of its "A Garden with Wings" exhibit this Saturday with Pollinator Palooza, a full day of gardens, butterflies, and native plant education from 10am to 5pm. It's a genuinely beautiful property and a good slow-morning kind of outing. This one skews toward nature lovers, gardeners, and anyone who wants a reason to be outside without a crowd pressing in on all sides. The anniversary makes this year's edition worth noting. Check the Smith-Gilbert Gardens site for admission details.
scoring_notes: The 10-year anniversary of the signature exhibit gives this recurring event a genuine uniqueness bump. Skews toward the gardening and nature-curious segment of East Cobb's audience, which is real but not the majority. Good for the right reader, not a universal crowd-pleaser.

blurb: This one is right in your backyard and the program is stacked. The night opens with John Williams' "ET: Adventures on Earth" and John Powell's "How to Train Your Dragon" before closing with Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique, one of the most dramatic pieces in the orchestral canon. If you've got a kid who grew up on those films, this is an easy sell for a family night out. If you're past that stage, the Berlioz alone is worth the drive to Shallowford Road. Lassiter Concert Hall is a great room. Grab tickets early.
scoring_notes: The film score opening acts make this accessible for families with teens, while the Berlioz headline appeals to the educated, culturally curious segment of East Cobb's empty nester population. Lassiter Concert Hall is a known local venue. Good cross-demographic appeal for this specific audience mix.

blurb: The 40th annual Glover Park Concert Series kicks off this Friday on Marietta Square. It's free, it's outside, and it's one of those summer rituals that East Cobb families and empty nesters both show up for year after year. Grab dinner on the Square before the music starts at 8, find a good spot on the lawn, and let the night handle itself. It's been running for four decades for a reason. Check the Wellstar Health System concert page for the full 2026 lineup.
scoring_notes: Free outdoor concert on Marietta Square is deeply local and appeals across the age spectrum of East Cobb's audience. The 40th anniversary angle adds a uniqueness hook that a typical week in the series wouldn't have. Strong audience match, moderate uniqueness boosted by the milestone year.

blurb: If "All for You" was in your regular rotation in the late 90s, Sister Hazel is playing City Green this Friday and it's going to feel like a very good flashback. The Gainesville, FL rock band went platinum and never really went away — they've been touring steadily for decades and put on a tight, crowd-pleasing show. This is the first City Green Live of the summer season, which means the crowd will be loose, the vibe will be easy, and everyone will know the words. Grab tickets before the week gets away from you.
scoring_notes: Sister Hazel peaked in the late 90s and early 2000s, which maps almost perfectly to the musical nostalgia window for a median-age-42 East Cobb audience. Outdoor concert format is ideal for this demographic. High audience match, solid uniqueness as a one-night show, excellent demographic fit.

blurb: Twenty-five of Sandy Springs' most talked-about restaurants, all in one place, one night only. Food That Rocks is essentially a progressive dinner at City Green — you graze your way through dishes and cocktails from spots you've been meaning to try, without committing to a full sit-down at any of them. It's a genuinely fun format, and it tends to sell out. If you're looking for a date night or a reason to get out with friends over the long weekend stretch, this is the one to put on the calendar. Check the Sandy Springs events page for tickets and the full restaurant lineup.
scoring_notes: One-night-only culinary event perfectly calibrated for East Cobb's income level and social lifestyle. Appeals to both couples and friend groups, skews adult, and the restaurant-tasting format is a proven hit with educated, food-curious suburban audiences. High uniqueness because it's a single annual event, not a recurring series.
